premise: I poured my entire $175,000 widow’s pension into a florist shop for my son and daughter-in-law with a handshake promise of 40% ownership, but within a year they secretly sold the most valuable inventory, drained the business account, and filed for divorce—she vanished with the cash into a luxury condo while I was left holding an empty LLC. I traced the funds, sued for conversion, won a $210,000 judgment, and garnished her new real-estate agent commissions until I recouped every cent plus interest, then sent her employer the court transcripts, causing her to lose her license for ethical violations.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Son And DIL Stole My $175K Pension, Drained My Shop. So I Let The State Take
